summary_zh: 启动并交互 AI agent hosts。

flags.name: host
flags.desc_zh: Start a local agent host server
flags.raw: Start a local agent host server

flags.name: ps
flags.desc_zh: List active sessions on a running agent host
flags.raw: List active sessions on a running agent host

flags.name: stop
flags.desc_zh: Cancel the active turn of a session
flags.raw: Cancel the active turn of a session

flags.name: kill
flags.desc_zh: Forcefully kill the running agent host process tree
flags.raw: Forcefully kill the running agent host process tree

flags.name: logs
flags.desc_zh: Stream live session events
flags.raw: Stream live session events

flags.name: help
flags.desc_zh: Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)
flags.raw: Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

flags.name: --host
flags.desc_zh: --host <HOST> Host the agent host should bind on. Defaults to 'localhost'. Pass `0.0.0.0` to expose the agent host on all interfaces (paired with a connection token unless `--without-connection-token` is set)
flags.raw: --host <HOST> Host the agent host should bind on. Defaults to 'localhost'. Pass `0.0.0.0` to expose the agent host on all interfaces (paired with a connection token unless `--without-connection-token` is set)

flags.name: --port
flags.desc_zh: --port <PORT> Port the agent host should bind on. If 0 (the default) the OS picks a free ephemeral port; the chosen port is recorded in the agent host lockfile [default: 0]
flags.raw: --port <PORT> Port the agent host should bind on. If 0 (the default) the OS picks a free ephemeral port; the chosen port is recorded in the agent host lockfile [default: 0]

flags.name: --connection-token
flags.desc_zh: --connection-token <CONNECTION_TOKEN> A secret that must be included with all requests
flags.raw: --connection-token <CONNECTION_TOKEN> A secret that must be included with all requests

flags.name: --connection-token-file
flags.desc_zh: --connection-token-file <CONNECTION_TOKEN_FILE> A file containing a secret that must be included with all requests
flags.raw: --connection-token-file <CONNECTION_TOKEN_FILE> A file containing a secret that must be included with all requests

flags.name: --without-connection-token
flags.desc_zh: --without-connection-token Run without a connection token. Only use this if the connection is secured by other means
flags.raw: --without-connection-token Run without a connection token. Only use this if the connection is secured by other means

flags.name: --server-data-dir
flags.desc_zh: --server-data-dir <SERVER_DATA_DIR> Specifies the directory that server data is kept in
flags.raw: --server-data-dir <SERVER_DATA_DIR> Specifies the directory that server data is kept in

flags.name: --replace
flags.desc_zh: --replace Stop any agent host already running on this machine and start a fresh one. Without this flag, the command reuses an existing live supervisor when its configuration is compatible, and errors out when the requested `--host` / `--port` / `--connection-token` differ from what's already running
flags.raw: --replace Stop any agent host already running on this machine and start a fresh one. Without this flag, the command reuses an existing live supervisor when its configuration is compatible, and errors out when the requested `--host` / `--port` / `--connection-token` differ from what's already running

flags.name: --tunnel
flags.desc_zh: --tunnel Expose the agent host over a dev tunnel
flags.raw: --tunnel Expose the agent host over a dev tunnel

flags.name: --name
flags.desc_zh: --name <NAME> Sets the machine name for the tunnel
flags.raw: --name <NAME> Sets the machine name for the tunnel

flags.name: --random-name
flags.desc_zh: --random-name Randomly name the machine for the tunnel
flags.raw: --random-name Randomly name the machine for the tunnel

flags.name: -h --help
flags.desc_zh: -h, --help Print help
flags.raw: -h, --help Print help

flags.name: --cli-data-dir
flags.desc_zh: Directory where CLI metadata should be stored [env: VSCODE_CLI_DATA_DIR=]
flags.raw: Directory where CLI metadata should be stored [env: VSCODE_CLI_DATA_DIR=]

flags.name: --verbose
flags.desc_zh: Print verbose output (implies --wait)
flags.raw: Print verbose output (implies --wait)

flags.name: --log
flags.desc_zh: Log level to use [possible values: trace, debug, info, warn, error, critical, off]
flags.raw: Log level to use [possible values: trace, debug, info, warn, error, critical, off]

raw:
Manage agent host sessions

Usage: code-tunnel agent [OPTIONS] [COMMAND]

Commands:
  host  Start a local agent host server
  ps    List active sessions on a running agent host
  stop  Cancel the active turn of a session
  kill  Forcefully kill the running agent host process tree
  logs  Stream live session events
  help  Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
      --host <HOST>
          Host the agent host should bind on. Defaults to 'localhost'. Pass `0.0.0.0` to expose the agent host on all interfaces (paired with a connection token unless `--without-connection-token` is set)
      --port <PORT>
          Port the agent host should bind on. If 0 (the default) the OS picks a free ephemeral port; the chosen port is recorded in the agent host lockfile [default: 0]
      --connection-token <CONNECTION_TOKEN>
          A secret that must be included with all requests
      --connection-token-file <CONNECTION_TOKEN_FILE>
          A file containing a secret that must be included with all requests
      --without-connection-token
          Run without a connection token. Only use this if the connection is secured by other means
      --server-data-dir <SERVER_DATA_DIR>
          Specifies the directory that server data is kept in
      --replace
          Stop any agent host already running on this machine and start a fresh one. Without this flag, the command reuses an existing live supervisor when its configuration is compatible, and errors out when the requested `--host` / `--port` / `--connection-token` differ from what's already running
      --tunnel
          Expose the agent host over a dev tunnel
      --name <NAME>
          Sets the machine name for the tunnel
      --random-name
          Randomly name the machine for the tunnel
  -h, --help
          Print help

GLOBAL OPTIONS:
      --cli-data-dir <CLI_DATA_DIR>  Directory where CLI metadata should be stored [env: VSCODE_CLI_DATA_DIR=]
      --verbose                      Print verbose output (implies --wait)
      --log <level>                  Log level to use [possible values: trace, debug, info, warn, error, critical, off]

summary_zh: 在当前工作目录传入 prompt 并运行 chat 会话。

flags.name: -m --mode
flags.desc_zh: The mode to use for the chat session. Available options: 'ask', 'edit', 'agent', or the identifier of a custom mode. Defaults to 'agent'.
flags.raw: The mode to use for the chat session. Available options: 'ask', 'edit', 'agent', or the identifier of a custom mode. Defaults to 'agent'.

flags.name: -a --add-file
flags.desc_zh: Add files as context to the chat session.
flags.raw: Add files as context to the chat session.

flags.name: --maximize
flags.desc_zh: Maximize the chat session view.
flags.raw: Maximize the chat session view.

flags.name: -r --reuse-window
flags.desc_zh: Force to use the last active window for the chat session.
flags.raw: Force to use the last active window for the chat session.

flags.name: -n --new-window
flags.desc_zh: Force to open an empty window for the chat session.
flags.raw: Force to open an empty window for the chat session.

flags.name: --profile
flags.desc_zh: --profile <profileName> Opens the provided folder or workspace with the given profile and associates the profile with the workspace. If the profile does not exist, a new empty one is created.
flags.raw: --profile <profileName> Opens the provided folder or workspace with the given profile and associates the profile with the workspace. If the profile does not exist, a new empty one is created.
flags.args: <profileName> Opens the provided folder or workspace with the given

raw:
Visual Studio Code 1.126.0

Usage: code chat [options] [prompt]

To read from stdin, append '-' (e.g. 'ps aux | grep code | code chat <prompt> -')

Options
  -m --mode <mode>        The mode to use for the chat session. Available
                          options: 'ask', 'edit', 'agent', or the identifier of
                          a custom mode. Defaults to 'agent'.
  -a --add-file <path>    Add files as context to the chat session.
  --maximize              Maximize the chat session view.
  -r --reuse-window       Force to use the last active window for the chat
                          session.
  -n --new-window         Force to open an empty window for the chat session.
  --profile <profileName> Opens the provided folder or workspace with the given
                          profile and associates the profile with the
                          workspace. If the profile does not exist, a new empty
                          one is created.

summary_zh: 启动一个在浏览器中显示编辑器 UI 的服务。

flags.name: --host
flags.desc_zh: --host <HOST> Host to listen on, defaults to 'localhost'
flags.raw: --host <HOST> Host to listen on, defaults to 'localhost'

flags.name: --socket-path
flags.desc_zh: --socket-path <SOCKET_PATH>
flags.raw: --socket-path <SOCKET_PATH>

flags.name: --port
flags.desc_zh: --port <PORT> Port to listen on. If 0 is passed a random free port is picked [default: 8000]
flags.raw: --port <PORT> Port to listen on. If 0 is passed a random free port is picked [default: 8000]

flags.name: --connection-token
flags.desc_zh: --connection-token <CONNECTION_TOKEN> A secret that must be included with all requests
flags.raw: --connection-token <CONNECTION_TOKEN> A secret that must be included with all requests

flags.name: --connection-token-file
flags.desc_zh: --connection-token-file <CONNECTION_TOKEN_FILE> A file containing a secret that must be included with all requests
flags.raw: --connection-token-file <CONNECTION_TOKEN_FILE> A file containing a secret that must be included with all requests

flags.name: --without-connection-token
flags.desc_zh: --without-connection-token Run without a connection token. Only use this if the connection is secured by other means
flags.raw: --without-connection-token Run without a connection token. Only use this if the connection is secured by other means

flags.name: --accept-server-license-terms
flags.desc_zh: --accept-server-license-terms If set, the user accepts the server license terms and the server will be started without a user prompt
flags.raw: --accept-server-license-terms If set, the user accepts the server license terms and the server will be started without a user prompt

flags.name: --server-base-path
flags.desc_zh: --server-base-path <SERVER_BASE_PATH> Specifies the path under which the web UI and the code server is provided
flags.raw: --server-base-path <SERVER_BASE_PATH> Specifies the path under which the web UI and the code server is provided

flags.name: --server-data-dir
flags.desc_zh: --server-data-dir <SERVER_DATA_DIR> Specifies the directory that server data is kept in
flags.raw: --server-data-dir <SERVER_DATA_DIR> Specifies the directory that server data is kept in

flags.name: --default-folder
flags.desc_zh: --default-folder <DEFAULT_FOLDER> The workspace folder to open when no input is specified in the browser URL
flags.raw: --default-folder <DEFAULT_FOLDER> The workspace folder to open when no input is specified in the browser URL

flags.name: --default-workspace
flags.desc_zh: --default-workspace <DEFAULT_WORKSPACE> The workspace to open when no input is specified in the browser URL
flags.raw: --default-workspace <DEFAULT_WORKSPACE> The workspace to open when no input is specified in the browser URL

flags.name: --disable-telemetry
flags.desc_zh: --disable-telemetry Disables telemetry
flags.raw: --disable-telemetry Disables telemetry

flags.name: --commit-id
flags.desc_zh: --commit-id <COMMIT_ID> Use a specific commit SHA for the client
flags.raw: --commit-id <COMMIT_ID> Use a specific commit SHA for the client

flags.name: -h --help
flags.desc_zh: -h, --help Print help
flags.raw: -h, --help Print help

flags.name: --cli-data-dir
flags.desc_zh: Directory where CLI metadata should be stored [env: VSCODE_CLI_DATA_DIR=]
flags.raw: Directory where CLI metadata should be stored [env: VSCODE_CLI_DATA_DIR=]

flags.name: --verbose
flags.desc_zh: Print verbose output (implies --wait)
flags.raw: Print verbose output (implies --wait)

flags.name: --log
flags.desc_zh: Log level to use [possible values: trace, debug, info, warn, error, critical, off]
flags.raw: Log level to use [possible values: trace, debug, info, warn, error, critical, off]

raw:
Runs a local web version of Visual Studio Code

Usage: code-tunnel serve-web [OPTIONS]

Options:
      --host <HOST>
          Host to listen on, defaults to 'localhost'
      --socket-path <SOCKET_PATH>
          
      --port <PORT>
          Port to listen on. If 0 is passed a random free port is picked [default: 8000]
      --connection-token <CONNECTION_TOKEN>
          A secret that must be included with all requests
      --connection-token-file <CONNECTION_TOKEN_FILE>
          A file containing a secret that must be included with all requests
      --without-connection-token
          Run without a connection token. Only use this if the connection is secured by other means
      --accept-server-license-terms
          If set, the user accepts the server license terms and the server will be started without a user prompt
      --server-base-path <SERVER_BASE_PATH>
          Specifies the path under which the web UI and the code server is provided
      --server-data-dir <SERVER_DATA_DIR>
          Specifies the directory that server data is kept in
      --default-folder <DEFAULT_FOLDER>
          The workspace folder to open when no input is specified in the browser URL
      --default-workspace <DEFAULT_WORKSPACE>
          The workspace to open when no input is specified in the browser URL
      --disable-telemetry
          Disables telemetry
      --commit-id <COMMIT_ID>
          Use a specific commit SHA for the client
  -h, --help
          Print help

GLOBAL OPTIONS:
      --cli-data-dir <CLI_DATA_DIR>  Directory where CLI metadata should be stored [env: VSCODE_CLI_DATA_DIR=]
      --verbose                      Print verbose output (implies --wait)
      --log <level>                  Log level to use [possible values: trace, debug, info, warn, error, critical, off]
